title: Gyoza Wrappers
description:
INGREDIENTS
* 4 cups all-purpose flour (560g)
* 2 cups boiling water (480g)
* ½ teaspoon coarse kosher salt

INSTRUCTIONS
1. Combine flour and salt in a large bowl. Stir in boiling water until a shaggy dough forms. Let it cool for a few minutes to avoid burning yourself.
2. Transfer to a floured work surface and knead for 5 minutes until smooth. Cover with plastic wrap and set aside.
3. Pinch off a tennis ball-sized dough and place it on a surface dusted with flour. Use a rolling pin to roll it out into a sheet about 1/16 inch thick.
4. Use a gyoza cutter or cookie cutter to cut out circles, then layer them on a floured surface, separating each layer with additional flour so they don't stick.
5. Squeeze the dough trimmings into a smaller ball, then repeat the rolling and circle-cutting to make more wrappers. Repeat until the dough is too small to form a wrapper.
6. Repeat with the rest of the dough.

title: Wonton Wrappers
description:
Ingredients
* 2 cups all purpose flour (240 g, 8.5 oz)
* 1 teaspoon salt
* 1 egg yolk
* ½ cup cold water for kneading
* ½ cup cornstarch (or arrowroot powder)

Instructions
1. Mix flour and salt in a large bowl.
2. Make a well in the center of the flour.
3. Add the egg yolk to the well and mix it with the flour using a spoon or your clean fingertips.
4. Add cold water (approx ½ cup) little by little and mix everything together with a spoon (or your hands), until the mixture begins to come together and a soft dough is made. You can add more water if required.
5. Turn the dough onto the counter and knead for 5-6 minutes using the heels of your hands. Kneading helps to form gluten and makes the dough smooth and elastic. This is a very important step to getting perfect wonton sheets.
6. The dough should be firm but smooth when you are done. If your dough is too tacky, add a little extra flour to your counter and knead again.
7. Note – You can use a stand mixer fitted with a dough attachment to mix and knead.
8. Cover the dough with a damp towel and let it rest on the counter for 30 minutes.
9. Knead once again for 2 minutes and divide it into 2 equal parts.
10. Take out one part and cover the rest with the towel.
11. Lightly dust your countertop with cornstarch (NOT flour), and roll as thin as you can into a rough rectangle. Thick wrappers produce an overly doughy result that can overwhelm the filling.
12. Cut into 4-inch squares (using a knife and ruler) or in circles (using a round cookie cutter).
13. Dust the wrappers generously with cornstarch to stop them from sticking to each other.
14. Stack them and wrap them in cling film. Keep the covered stacks in a ziplock bag and freeze for up to 3 months.
15. Repeat with the remaining dough.
16. Thaw the wrappers overnight (or 2-3 hours) in the fridge before using them.
17. You can also cut these into rounds for dumplings.

title: Spring Rolls Wrappers
description:
Ingredients
* 2 cups, all-purpose flour(maida)
* 1 cup, cornflour
* ½ teaspoon salt
* 3 & ½ cup, water
* Oil for greasing

Instructions
1. In a large bowl, add flour, cornflour, salt and mix well
2. Add water and whisk well to make lump free batter
3. Now, take a flat non-stick griddle/pan and heat it on gas stove on very low heat.
4. Lightly grease the pan with oil. (I poured couple of drops of oil and spread it with tissue towel)
5. Now, start brushing the batter on the greased pan to make square shape.
6. Do not make too many coats to make a thick wrapper and make sure that wrappers are free of any pits.
7. Allow it to cook for few minutes, when wrapper is cooked well, it will start leaving the edges ensuring you to peel of the wrapper from the griddle or take them out using spatula. Carefully, take it off the pan and place on the tray, keep it covered with damp kitchen cloth, please do not allow the sheets to come in contact with air or the sheets will get dry.
8. Also, do not increase the heat, maintain the hotness of the pan at low heat only.
9. Now, clean the griddle, grease it again and proceed with brushing the batter.
10. Repeat the process until done.
11. Once you are done with making all the wrappers.
12. Now, to store the wrappers in the freezer, brush each sheet with oil and stack them.
13. Cover with parchment paper and keep it in zip lock. Greasing of sheets will make it easier to separate after thawing. You can store the sheets in batches, this will allow you the easy access to the amount required in one go. Just thaw at room temperature and proceed with making of spring rolls.

title: Fresh Lumpia Wrapper
description:
Ingredients
* 1 cup all-purpose flour
* 2 eggs
* 2 tablespoons canola oil
* 1/8 teaspoon salt
* 1 1/2 cups water
* 1/4 cup cooking oil FOR BRUSHING PAN

Instructions
1. In a medium sized bowl, mix all of the Fresh Lumpia wrapper ingredient and mix until there are no lumps. Sift mixture into a bowl and cook wrapper.
2. TO cook wrapper, IT IS A MUST THAT YOU USE A NON STICK PAN.
3. Heat non stick pan in a low heat and brush pan with oil. MAKE SURE THAT THE PAN has no excess oil in it. If it does, wipe the oil with paper towel. GOAL HERE IS THAT PAN HAS JUST very SMALL AMOUNT OF COOKING OIL. If you have excess oil, the wrapper will turn dark. SO I RECOMMEND WIPING PAN WITH PAPER TOWEL AFTER EACH OIL ADDITION.
4. When oil is hot, pour in 1/3 cup of wrapper mixture and very gently distribute mixture throughout the pan surface. OR, YOU CAN JUST LET THE MIXTURE NATURALLY SPREAD IN THE PAN. Cook until mixture is dry and just shake pan to release the wrapper and slide wrapper in a flat plate. Do the same to the rest of the mixture and let wrapper cool.

title: Dave's Hot Chicken Sauce
description:
INGREDIENTS
* 1/2 cup Mayonnaise Full-Fat recommended
* 2 tbsp Ketchup
* 1 tbsp Hot Sauce
* 1 tbsp Cayenne Pepper (adjust for spice level)
* 1 tsp Smoked Paprika
* 1 tsp Garlic Powder
* 1 tsp Onion Powder
* 1 tbsp Honey (brown sugar as a substitute)
* 1 tbsp White Vinegar (apple cider vinegar as a substitute)
* 1/2 tsp Black Pepper
* Salt (to taste)

INSTRUCTIONS
1. In a medium bowl, combine mayonnaise, ketchup, and hot sauce. Mix well until smooth.
2. Add cayenne pepper, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, black pepper, and salt. Stir until all spices are fully incorporated.
3. Add honey and white vinegar, then mix again until smooth and creamy.
4. Taste and adjust seasoning by changing the amount of cayenne pepper to adjust the spice level as needed.
5. Transfer to an airtight container and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to let the flavors meld.

title: Nando's Peri Peri Sauce
description:
INGREDIENTS
* 3-8 Thai red chilies (depending on spice preference; 3 for medium; 8 for extra hot)
* 1 medium red bell pepper
* ½ small onion
* 1 lemon (zested and juiced)
* 1-2 tablespoons white vinegar(depending on preference)
* ½ cup olive oil (or canola oil)
* 9 cloves garlic (about 3 tablespoons)
* 2 teaspoons smoked paprika
* 1 tablespoon dried oregano
* 1 tablespoon coarse kosher salt

INSTRUCTIONS
1. Add all ingredients to a food processor or blender. Blend for 30-60 seconds depending on the texture you want. I like the sauce to be a bit chunkier as the chunky bits get nice and caramelized when roasted.
2. Check for spiciness. If you want it hotter, blend in more chili peppers.
3. Transfer to a jar and refrigerate until ready for use. Depending on the freshness of your ingredients, the sauce can last up to a week in the fridge.

title: Cane’s Sauce
description:
INGREDIENTS
* 4.4 oz mayonnaise (1/2 cup)
* 2.6 oz ketchup (3 Tbsp)
* 1.5 Tbsp Worcestershire sauce
* 1 tsp garlic powder
* 1/2 tsp black pepper (coarse)
* 1/2 tsp salt

INSTRUCTIONS
1. Place all the ingredients into a bowl and stir to combine.
2. Cover and refrigerate for at least 6 hours before serving.
3. Stir before serving and enjoy with chicken, fries, or veggies.

title: McDonald’s Spicy Pepper Sauce
description:
Ingredients:
* 1 cup Creamy Mayonnaise
* 2 tsp Red Tabasco Hot Sauce
* 4 tsp Hot Sauce
* ½ cup Dill Pickle Juice
* 4 tsp Tomato Ketchup
* 4 tsp French Dressing
* 4 tsp Yellow Mustard
* 1 tsp Chili Powder
* 2 tsp Reaper Sauce
* 4 tbsp Wing Master’s Habanero Mango Sauce
* 1 tsp Garlic Powder
* ⅓ tsp Cumin Powder
* 2 tsp Crushed Red Pepper Flakes
* 1 teaspoon Lime Juice
* 4-6 tbsp Dill Pickle Juice
* 4 tsp Fresh Black Pepper

Instructions:
1. In a medium bowl, mix black pepper, cayenne pepper and crushed red pepper flakes, garlic powder and onion powder.
2. Add mayonnaise to the spice mixture and stir until well combined.
3. Gradually add in Habanero Mango Sauce, Tabasco Hot Sauce, and Hot Sauce. Continue stirring until all ingredients are well incorporated.
4. Add in ketchup and mix well.
5. Next, add French Dressing and mix until smooth.
6. Then add Yellow Mustard and mix until well combined.
7. Add in dill pickle juice, depending on desired tanginess level, and mix well.
8. Finally, squeeze in the juice from lime and mix until all ingredients are well incorporated.
9. Store the sauce in an airtight container in the refrigerator until ready to use.

title: Oreo Tres leches
description:
INGREDIENTS
* 1 box of white cake mix
* 3 large eggs
* 1 cup of milk
* 1 can (14 oz) of sweetened condensed milk
* 1 can (12 oz) of evaporated milk
* 1 cup of heavy whipping cream, divided
* 1 cup of crushed Oreo cookies
* 1 cup of powdered sugar
* 1 teaspoon of vanilla extract
* Whole Oreo cookies for garnish

INSTRUCTIONS
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C) and prepare a 9×13 inch baking dish by lightly greasing it.
2. In a large bowl, mix the white cake mix, eggs, and 1 cup of milk according to the package instructions. Pour the batter into the prepared baking dish and bake for 30-35 minutes or until a toothpick comes out clean.
3. Let the cake cool completely in the pan for about 30-45 minutes at room temperature.
4. In a separate bowl, whisk together the sweetened condensed milk, evaporated milk, and ½ cup of heavy cream until well combined.
5. Poke holes all over the cooled cake using a fork or skewer.
6. Pour the milk mixture over the cake slowly, allowing it to soak in completely.
7. In another bowl, whip the remaining ½ cup of heavy cream with powdered sugar and vanilla extract until soft peaks form.
8. Fold in the crushed Oreo cookies into the whipped cream mixture.
9. Spread the Oreo whipped cream over the soaked cake evenly.
10. Garnish with whole Oreo cookies on top and refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving.

title: Mango Tres Leches
description:
Ingredients
* 1 cup all-purpose flour
* 1 ½ teaspoons baking powder
* ¼ teaspoon salt
* 3 large eggs, separated
* ¾ cup granulated sugar, divided
* 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
* ½ cup whole milk

Milk soak:
* 1 (12 oz) can evaporated milk
* 1 (14 oz) can sweetened condensed milk
* ½ cup heavy cream

Mango layer and topping:
* 2 cups fresh ripe mango, diced and divided
* 1 cup heavy cream
* 2 tablespoons powdered sugar
* ½ teaspoon vanilla extract

Instructions
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour an 8×8-inch baking pan.
2. In a bowl, whisk flour, baking powder, and salt. In a separate bowl, beat egg yolks with ½ cup sugar and vanilla until pale and thick. Stir in milk. Fold in dry ingredients gently.
3. In another bowl, beat egg whites until soft peaks form. Gradually add remaining ¼ cup sugar and beat until stiff peaks hold. Fold whites gently into batter.
4. Pour batter into prepared pan and bake for 25–30 minutes, or until a toothpick comes out clean. Let cool slightly.
5. Mix evaporated milk, sweetened condensed milk, and heavy cream. Poke holes all over warm cake and pour soak mixture over it. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
6. Blend 1 cup diced mango with 1 tablespoon of heavy cream until smooth. Spread mango purée over soaked cake.
7. Whip 1 cup heavy cream with powdered sugar and vanilla until stiff peaks form. Spread or pipe over mango layer.
8. Top with remaining diced mango and chill for another 30 minutes. Cut and serve cold.

title: DULCE DE LECHE TRES LECHES CAKE
description:
Ingredients:
* 1 cup all-purpose flour
* 1 teaspoon baking powder
* 1/2 teaspoon baking soda
* 1/4 teaspoon salt
* 4 large eggs, separated
* 1 cup granulated sugar, divided
* 1/2 cup whole milk
* 1/2 cup vegetable oil
* 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
* 1 can (12 oz) evaporated milk
* 1 can (14 oz) sweetened condensed milk
* 1 cup heavy cream
* 1 cup dulce de leche for topping

Instructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Prepare a 9-inch round cake pan by greasing and flouring it or line it with parchment paper for easier removal.
2. In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. This will ensure the dry ingredients are well combined for a fluffy cake.
3. In a large mixing bowl, beat the egg yolks with 3/4 cup of granulated sugar until the mixture is light and fluffy. This should take about 4-5 minutes. Add the milk, vegetable oil, and vanilla extract, mixing until well incorporated.
4. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture, stirring gently until just combined. Do not over-mix; a few lumps are fine!
5. In a separate bowl, beat the egg whites until stiff peaks form. Slowly add the remaining 1/4 cup of sugar while continuing to beat until glossy and stiff peaks hold their shape.
6. Carefully fold the beaten egg whites into the cake batter in three additions, ensuring not to deflate the mixture. This will give your cake a light and airy texture.
7. Pour the batter into the prepared cake pan and smooth the top with a spatula. Bake for 25-30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
8. While the cake is baking, prepare the tres leches mixture. In a bowl, combine evaporated milk, sweetened condensed milk, and heavy cream. Whisk until smooth.
9. Once the cake is out of the oven, allow it to cool for about 10 minutes before inverting onto a serving plate. Use a skewer to poke holes all over the top of the cake, allowing the milk mixture to soak in.
10. Slowly pour the tres leches mixture over the cake, ensuring it absorbs as much as possible. Let the cake cool completely at room temperature.
11. Once the cake has cooled, spread a generous layer of dulce de leche on top for that sweet, caramelized finish. You can also drizzle some extra on slices when serving.
12. Chill the cake in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before serving to ensure the flavors blend beautifully and the cake becomes even more moist.

title: UBE TRES LECHES CAKE
description:
Ingredients
For the Cake
* 2 cups All-Purpose Flour
* 2 teaspoons Baking Powder
* ½ teaspoon Salt
* 4 large Eggs
* ½ cup Whole Milk
* ¾ cup Granulated Sugar
* 1 teaspoon Ube Extract
* ½ cup Ube Halaya (ube jam)
For the Milk Soak
* 1 can Evaporated Milk
* 1 can Condensed Milk
* 1 can Coconut Milk
For the Topping
* 1 cup Heavy Whipping Cream
* ¼ cup Granulated Sugar

Instructions
1. Preheat the Oven to 350°F (180°C) and grease a 9x13-inch baking dish.
2. In a medium bowl, sift together 2 cups of all-purpose flour, 2 teaspoons of baking powder, and ½ teaspoon of salt.
3. In a large mixing bowl, beat together 4 large egg yolks and ¾ cup of granulated sugar until fluffy and pale.
4. Fold in ½ cup of ube halaya, 1 teaspoon of ube extract, and ½ cup of whole milk.
5. Gently fold the wet egg yolk mixture into the bowl of sifted dry ingredients until combined.
6. In a clean bowl, beat the 4 large egg whites with ¼ cup of sugar until stiff peaks form.
7. Carefully fold the whipped egg whites into the cake batter in three additions.
8. Pour the batter into the prepared baking dish and bake for 30 minutes or until a toothpick comes out clean.
9. While the cake cools, whisk together 1 can of evaporated milk, 1 can of condensed milk, 1 can of coconut milk, and 1 teaspoon of ube extract.
10. Poke holes in the cooled cake and pour the milk mixture evenly over the cake.
11. Allow the cake to sit at room temperature for 20 minutes, then refrigerate for at least 8 hours.
12. To prepare the topping, beat together 1 cup of heavy whipping cream, ¼ cup of coconut milk, 2 tablespoons of sugar, and 1 teaspoon of ube extract until soft peaks form.
13. Spread the whipped topping over the soaked cake.

title: BAKED LEMON PEPPER CHICKEN WINGS
description:
Ingredients
* 1.5 – 2 pounds chicken wings (about 16-20 wings)
* 3 Tablespoons olive oil
* 4 Tablespoons salted butter
* 1-2 Tablespoons honey (optional)
* 2 Tablespoons lemon pepper seasoning
* 2 teaspoons freshly squeezed lemon juice
* 1 teaspoon lemon zest

Instructions
1. Preheat oven to 425˚F.
2. Set wings on top of a cooling rack set atop a rimmed baking sheet. Brush each wing with olive oil on both sides.
3. Bake for 35-40 minutes (depending on the size of your wings), until they’re golden brown and crispy. The internal temperature should read 165˚F.
4. In a small pot melt butter (or melt butter in the microwave) then pour into a larger bowl and whisk in the honey, lemon pepper seasoning, lemon juice, and lemon zest.
5. Add wings to the bowl, and toss until they’re evenly coated.
6. Optional: serve garnished with a crack or two of black pepper, a little more lemon zest, and some lemon slices on the side.

title: Crispy Baked Buffalo Wings
description:
INGREDIENTS
* 2 pounds chicken wings (split into flats and drums)
* 1 tablespoon oil
* 2 teaspoons baking powder
* 1 teaspoon salt
* 1 teaspoon garlic powder
* ½ teaspoon black pepper
* ¼ cup Franks RedHot Original Sauce
* ¼ cup melted butter
* 1 tablespoon brown sugar

INSTRUCTIONS
1. Preheat your oven to 450°F. Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and place a wire rack on top.
2. Pat the wings dry with paper towels (this helps them crisp up in the oven).
3. In a large bowl, toss the wings with oil, baking powder, salt, garlic powder, and black pepper.
4. Arrange the wings on the baking sheet with some space between each one.
5. Bake for 45-50 minutes, flipping once halfway through until golden and crispy.
6. Mix the Frank's RedHot original sauce, butter, and brown sugar in a large bowl. While the wings are still hot, add them to the bowl and toss until evenly coated. Serve with fresh veggies and your favorite dip.

title: Sweet and Spicy Chicken Wings
description:
INGREDIENTS
For the Wings:
* 2 pounds of chicken wings split into flats and drumettes
* 1 teaspoon salt
* ½ teaspoon black pepper
* 1 teaspoon garlic powder
* 1 teaspoon smoked paprika
For the Sweet and Spicy Glaze:
* ¼ cup honey
* ¼ cup brown sugar
* ¼ cup hot sauce choose your favorite brand for desired heat
* 1 tablespoon soy sauce
* 1 tablespoon apple cider vinegar
* ½ teaspoon cayenne pepperoptional, for extra heat
Optional Garnishes:
* Sliced green onions
* Sesame seeds
* Chopped fresh cilantro

INSTRUCTIONS
Prepare the Chicken Wings:
1. Rinse the chicken wings under cold water and pat them dry with paper towels. Removing excess moisture helps achieve crispy skin.
2. In a large bowl, toss the wings with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and smoked paprika to coat evenly.

Cook the Wings:
* Baking Method:
1. Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Place the wings on a wire rack set over a baking sheet lined with aluminum foil.
3. Bake for 40-45 minutes, flipping halfway through, until the wings are golden brown and crispy.
* Frying Method:
1. Heat oil in a deep fryer or heavy-bottomed pot to 375°F (190°C).
2. Fry the wings in batches for 8-10 minutes until golden and cooked through.
3. Remove and drain on paper towels.

Make the Sweet and Spicy Glaze:
1. In a small saucepan, combine honey, brown sugar, hot sauce, soy sauce, apple cider vinegar, and cayenne pepper.
2. Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the mixture starts to bubble and slightly thickens (about 3-5 minutes).

Toss the Wings in the Glaze:
1. Transfer the cooked wings to a large mixing bowl.
2. Pour the sweet and spicy glaze over the wings and toss until they are evenly coated.

title: Honey Barbecue Chicken Wings
description:
Ingredients
* 2 pounds chicken wings, separated into flats and drumettes
* 1 cup barbecue sauce
* 1/3 cup honey
* 1 tablespoon olive oil
* 1 teaspoon garlic powder
* 1 teaspoon smoked paprika
* 1/2 teaspoon salt
* 1/2 teaspoon black pepper

Instructions

1. Start by preheating your oven to 400°F (200°C). This temperature will help your wings turn out crispy and delicious!

2. Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil for easy cleanup. Place a wire rack on top. This allows air to flow around the wings for even cooking, resulting in a better texture.

3. In a large bowl, toss the chicken wings with olive oil, garlic powder, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper. Mix well until all the wings are evenly coated with the seasoning.

4. Place the seasoned wings on the wire rack in a single layer. Make sure they’re not touching each other, as this helps them cook evenly.

5. Put the baking sheet in the preheated oven and bake for 35-40 minutes. Flip the wings halfway through cooking to ensure they brown nicely on both sides.

6. While the wings are baking, mix together the barbecue sauce and honey in a separate bowl. This will be the sweet and sticky coating for your wings!

7. Once the wings are done baking, take them out of the oven. Generously brush both sides of the wings with the honey barbecue sauce you prepared.

8. Return the sauced wings to the oven and bake for an additional 5-7 minutes. This final bake will caramelize the sauce and give the wings that perfect glaze.

9. Once done, remove the wings from the oven and let them rest for a few minutes. If you like, serve them on a bed of fresh lettuce leaves for a nice presentation. Enjoy your sticky, sweet, and smoky honey barbecue chicken wings!

title: COCA COLA CHICKEN
description:
Ingredients
* 2 pounds chicken wings
* salt & pepper, to taste
* 2 tablespoons oil
* 1 can coca cola
* 3 stalks green onions, cut into thirds
* 1 tablespoon shaoxing wine
* 1 tablespoon dark soy sauce
* 2 tablespoons soy sauce, low sodium

Instructions
1. Pat dry the chicken wings with a paper towel. Season them generously with salt and pepper.
2. Heat up a tablespoon of oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Once the oil is hot, add the chicken wings in a single layer.
3. Sear the chicken wings on both sides for 5 to 6 minutes per side, or until the skin is golden. You can add more oil as needed.
4. After searing the wings on both sides, add the cola, green onions, shaoxing wine, soy sauce, and dark soy sauce to the skillet.
5. Simmer the mixture in the skillet for 15 to 20 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened to coat your chicken wings.
6. Discard the green onions and serve.

title: Fried Sticky Korean Chicken Wings
description:
Ingredients for Chicken Wings
* 2 lb chicken wings
* 1 tsp sea salt
* ½ tsp freshly ground black pepper
* ½ cup cornstarch
* ¼ cup all-purpose flour
* ½ tsp baking powder
* vegetable oil

Ingredients Sticky Korean Sauce
* 3 Tbsp gochujang hot sauce
* 3 Tbsp soy sauce
* ¼ cup honey
* 2 Tbsp brown sugar
* 2 Tbsp rice vinegar
* 3 cloves garlic, minced
* 1 tsp fresh ginger root, grated
* 1 tsp sesame oil
* ¼ cup water
* toasted sesame seeds (for garnish)

Instructions
1. Pat the chicken wings dry very well with paper towels to remove surface moisture.
2. Place them in a large bowl and season evenly with sea salt and black pepper.
3. In a separate bowl whisk together the cornstarch, all-purpose flour, and baking powder.
4. Add the wings and toss until every piece is well coated and no dry patches remain.
5. Pour vegetable oil into a heavy pot or deep pan so it is about 2 inches deep.
6. Heat the oil to 350°F over medium heat, using a thermometer for accuracy if possible.
7. Fry the wings in batches, without crowding, for 8 to 10 minutes until light golden and cooked through.
8. Transfer them to a wire rack set over a sheet pan to drain while you fry the remaining batches.
9. Increase the oil temperature slightly to about 375°F for the second fry. Return the wings to the hot oil in batches and fry 3 to 4 minutes until deeply golden and very crisp.
10. Drain again on the wire rack and keep warm.
Make the sticky Korean sauce:
1. While the wings fry, combine gochujang, soy sauce, honey, and brown sugar in a small saucepan.
2. Add the rice vinegar, garlic, ginger, sesame oil, and water, and stir until everything is evenly mixed.
3. Place the pan over medium heat and simmer 3 to 5 minutes, stirring often, until slightly thickened and glossy.
4. Transfer the hot wings to a large clean bowl and pour the warm sauce over them.
5. Toss gently with tongs until every wing is coated in a sticky, even layer of sauce.
6. Sprinkle the sticky wings with toasted sesame seeds and an optional shower of thinly sliced green onions.
7. Serve immediately while the coating stays crisp and the sauce is hot and glossy.

title: Peach Upside-Down Cake
description:
Ingredients
The Caramelized Topping
* 60g Unsalted Butter, melted
* 100g Light Brown Sugar, packed
* 3–4 large Fresh Peaches, firm but ripe, sliced into 1cm wedges
* 1/4 teaspoon Ground Ginger (optional, for a subtle warmth)

The Vanilla Sponge
* 190g All-Purpose Flour
* 150g Granulated Sugar
* 1.5 teaspoons Baking Powder
* 1/2 teaspoon Fine Sea Salt
* 115g Unsalted Butter, softened to room temperature
* 1 Large Egg, room temperature.
* 120ml Sour Cream(this provides the essential moisture and tang).
* 60ml Whole Milk
* 1 tablespoon Vanilla Bean Paste

Instructions
1. Preheat your oven to 175°C. Pour the melted butter into a 23cm (9-inch) round cake pan. Sprinkle the brown sugar and ginger evenly over the butter. Arrange the peach slices in a circular pattern, overlapping them slightly until the entire base of the pan is covered.

2. In a large bowl, beat the softened butter and granulated sugar until the mixture is pale and fluffy. Add the egg and vanilla bean paste, beating again until fully incorporated.
3. In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt. In a small jug, mix the sour cream and milk. Alternate adding the dry ingredients and the liquid mixture to the butter base, starting and ending with the flour. Mix until just combined; over-mixing will lead to a tough sponge.

4. Carefully dollop the thick batter over the peaches. Use an offset spatula to spread it gently to the edges, being careful not to disturb your peach arrangement. Bake for 35–45 minutes. The cake is done when the top is golden brown and a skewer inserted into the center comes out clean.

5. Let the cake cool in the pan for exactly 10 minutes. This allows the caramel to settle but prevents it from hardening and sticking. Run a knife around the edge. Place a serving plate over the pan and, in one confident motion, invert it. Leave the pan in place for a minute to let the juices drizzle down, then slowly lift it off.

title: Strawberry Upside Down Cake
description:
Ingredients
For the Topping
* 2 cups Fresh strawberries use
* 1/2 cup Brown sugar
* 1/4 cup Unsalted butter
For the Cake Batter
* 1 cup Granulated sugar
* 1/2 cup Unsalted butter softened
* 2 large Eggs
* 1 cup Milk
* 1 1/2 cups All-purpose flour
* 2 teaspoons Baking powder
* 1 teaspoon Vanilla extract
* 1/4 teaspoon Salt

Instructions
1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and grease a 9-inch round baking pan with 1/4 cup of unsalted butter.

2. Arrange the 2 cups of fresh strawberries evenly over the bottom of the greased pan. Sprinkle 1/2 cup of brown sugar on top.

3. Cream together 1/2 cup of softened unsalted butter and 1 cup of granulated sugar until light and fluffy.

4. Beat in 2 large eggs one at a time, then stir in 1 cup of milk and 1 teaspoon of vanilla extract.

5. Combine 1 1/2 cups of all-purpose flour, 2 teaspoons of baking powder, and 1/4 teaspoon of salt in another bowl.

6. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture, mixing until just combined.

7. Pour the batter over the arranged strawberries in the pan.

8. Bake for 35 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.

9. Cool the cake for a few minutes in the pan, then invert it onto a serving plate to showcase the strawberries.

title: BANANA FOSTER UPSIDE DOWN CAKE
description:
INGREDIENTS
* 3 ripe bananas, sliced
* 1 cup brown sugar, packed
* 1 cup butter, softened and divided
* 1 1/2 cups all purpose flour
* 1 1/2 teaspoons baking powder
* 1/4 teaspoon salt
* 2 large eggs
* 3/4 cup granulated sugar
* 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
* 1/2 cup milk

INSTRUCTIONS
1. Preheat your oven to 350 degrees and lightly grease a 9 inch cake pan.

2. Whisk flour, baking powder, and salt in one bowl. In another, cream butter and granulated sugar until fluffy, then add eggs and vanilla.

3. Mix dry ingredients into wet, alternating with milk until smooth.

4. Melt butter and brown sugar until bubbly, then pour into the cake pan.

5. Arrange banana slices neatly over the caramel layer.

6. Pour cake batter over the bananas and smooth the top.

7. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes until golden and set.

8. Let the cake rest for 10 minutes, then flip onto a serving plate.

title: Chocolate Cherry Upside Down Cake
description:
INGREDIENTS
* 1 cup pitted cherries (fresh or frozen)
* ½ cup unsalted butter, melted
* 1 cup packed brown sugar
* 1 cup all-purpose flour
* ½ cup unsweetened cocoa powder
* 1 cup granulated sugar
* 1 teaspoon baking powder
* ½ teaspoon baking soda
* ¼ teaspoon salt
* 2 large eggs
* 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
* ½ cup buttermilk
* Powdered sugar for dusting (optional)

INSTRUCTIONS
1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 9-inch round cake pan or use a springform pan for easy removal.

2. In a small bowl, mix the melted butter with brown sugar. Pour this mixture into the bottom of the prepared cake pan.

3. Evenly distribute the pitted cherries over the brown sugar mixture in the pan.

4. In a mixing bowl, sift together the flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.

5. In another bowl, whisk together the granulated sugar, eggs, vanilla extract, and buttermilk until well combined.

6. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet ingredients, stirring gently until everything is incorporated and smooth.

7. Carefully pour the chocolate batter over the cherries and the brown sugar mixture, spreading it evenly.

8. Place the cake in the preheated oven and bake for about 35-40 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.

9. Once baked, remove from the oven. Let it cool in the pan for about 15-20 minutes to set before inverting onto a serving plate.

10. Carefully turn the cake over onto a serving dish, allowing the cherries to rest on top.

11. For a lovely finishing touch, dust with powdered sugar before serving, if desired.

title: Wuhan Style: Hot Dry Noodles (Re Gan Mian)
description:
INGREDIENTS


150g   dried standard thick wheat noodles   (lo mein)


1 tbsp   toasted sesame oil  


3 tbsp   Chinese sesame paste   or tahini


4 cloves   garlic, crushed  


1 tbsp   groundnut oil  


2 tbsp   light soy sauce  


1 tbsp   dark soy sauce  


1 tbsp   Chinese black vinegar  


1/2 tsp   caster sugar  


1 tbsp   Chiu Chow chilli oil   with sediment


1/4 tsp   salt  


1 tbsp   boiling water  


4 tbsp   Sichuan preserved vegetables   (zha cai), chopped


2   spring onions   (green and white parts), thinly sliced


1  tbsp sesame seeds  


  Pickled Chillies  


INSTRUCTIONS

1. Cook the noodles   according to the instructions on the packet.   Drain   them in a colander and   dress with the toasted sesame oil  , mixing with your hands. Leave the noodles to   dry out in the colander   for at least 30 minutes to achieve the traditional chewy texture.

2. Combine all the sauce ingredients   in a bowl or jug and   whisk vigorously  . This includes the sesame paste, crushed garlic, groundnut oil, both soy sauces, black vinegar, caster sugar, chilli oil, salt, and the tablespoon of boiling water.

3. When you are ready to serve,   reheat the noodles   by placing them back in the colander and   pouring a kettleful of boiling water over them  .

4. Tip the noodles into a large bowl   and   pour the prepared sauce over them  . Mix the noodles thoroughly so they are completely coated in the spicy sauce.

5. Top the noodles with the garnish   of Sichuan preserved vegetables, thinly sliced spring onions, sesame seeds, and pickled chillies. Serve the dish immediately while hot.

title: Sichuan Dan Dan Noodles (Sichuan Dan Dan Mian)
description:
INGREDIENTS

200g   fresh Shanghai noodles   (cui mian) or 125g   dried standard wheat noodles   (lo mein)


1 tsp   groundnut oil   (for noodles)


4 tbsp   groundnut oil   (for stir-fry)


1 tbsp   Sichuan peppercorns  


200g   minced pork  


1cm piece   fresh root ginger  , peeled and finely chopped


2 cloves   garlic  , finely chopped


4 tbsp   Sichuan preserved vegetables   (zha cai), chopped


1 tbsp   Chinese rice wine   or dry sherry


1 tbsp   Chinese black vinegar  


1 tsp   chilli bean sauce  


1 tsp   caster sugar  


1 tbsp   light soy sauce  


1 tsp   dark soy sauce  


50ml   water  


Chilli Oil   (for garnish)

2   spring onions   (green and white parts), shredded


INSTRUCTIONS

1. Prepare the aromatic oil   by heating the 4 tablespoons of groundnut oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat. Add the Sichuan peppercorns and fry for 1–2 minutes until fragrant, then scoop out the peppercorns with a slotted spoon and discard.

2. Cook the meat mixture   by adding the minced pork, ginger, garlic, and preserved vegetables to the hot oil. Stir-fry for 3–4 minutes until the meat is no longer pink.

3. Incorporate the sauce seasonings   by adding the rice wine, black vinegar, chilli bean sauce, sugar, and both soy sauces. Mix thoroughly and stir-fry for 2–3 minutes, making sure to break up the meat into small pieces.

4. Simmer the topping   by adding the 50ml of water and bringing the mixture to a boil. Turn the heat down to medium-low and allow the sauce to simmer for 15 minutes.

5. Prepare the noodles   while the sauce is simmering. If using fresh Shanghai noodles, blanch them in just-boiled water for 5 minutes; if using dried noodles, cook according to the packet instructions. Drain the noodles and toss them with 1 teaspoon of groundnut oil to prevent sticking.

6. Assemble the dish   by dividing the noodles between bowls and topping them with the pork sauce, a lashing of chilli oil, and the shredded spring onions. Stir the noodles thoroughly before eating to ensure the sauce is evenly distributed.

title: Ants Climbing a Tree (Ma Yi Shang Shu)
description:
INGREDIENTS

200g   minced pork  

4   dried shiitake mushrooms  

200g   dried mung-bean thread noodles  

3 tbsp   groundnut oil  

3 cloves   garlic  , finely chopped

1cm piece   fresh root ginger  , peeled and finely chopped

1 stalk   Chinese celery  , thinly sliced

4   spring onions   (white parts finely chopped, green parts shredded)

300ml   chicken or vegetable stock  

1 tbsp   toasted sesame oil  

2   fresh red finger chillies  , chopped

For the pork marinade  : 1 tsp   dark soy sauce  , 2 tsp   light soy sauce  , 2 tbsp   chilli bean sauce  , 1 tsp   toasted sesame oil  , 2 tbsp   Chinese rice wine   or dry sherry, 2 tsp   golden caster sugar  , and a pinch of   cornflour  


INSTRUCTIONS


1. Prepare the pork   by combining the minced meat with all the marinade ingredients in a bowl. Mix the ingredients thoroughly and leave the meat to   marinate for at least 15 minutes  .

2. Rehydrate the mushrooms   by placing the dried shiitake in a bowl and generously covering them with just-boiled water. Leave them to soak for 15 minutes until soft, then drain the liquid and   slice the mushrooms   into thin strips.

3. Sauté the aromatics   by heating the groundnut oil in a wok or large frying pan. Add the garlic, ginger, Chinese celery, and the white parts of the spring onions, stir-frying until the mixture is   fragrant  .

4. Cook the meat sauce   by adding the marinated pork to the wok. Stir-fry for several minutes until the pork is cooked through, using your spoon to   break the meat into small flecks   that represent the "ants" in the dish's name.

5. Incorporate the noodles   by adding the sliced mushrooms and the stock to the wok and bringing the liquid to a boil. Add the dried mung-bean thread noodles and   simmer for about 5 minutes  , or until the noodles are soft and have absorbed the savory sauce.

6. Finalize the dish   by adding the shredded spring onion greens, the tablespoon of toasted sesame oil, and the chopped red chillies. Toss everything together once more to ensure the "ants" are clinging to the "twigs" (noodles) and   serve immediately   while hot.
